B.text: along the outer surface of each backbone

C.text: only at the start and end of each backbone

D.text: on one side of a backbone, without connecting to the opposite backbone

parsed.rationale: The image clearly shows the DNA double helix structure where the sugar-phosphate backbones form the outer rails. The nitrogen bases (colored blocks) are depicted connecting these two backbones, positioned in the interior space between them. This arrangement is fundamental to the DNA structure shown.
